paragraphs.text: As I mentioned when talking about the way we perceive the absence of God -- which is obviously not something objective but subjective -- unless of that we are aware that we are outside the kingdom of God, that we need to knock on a door to be allowed to enter, we can waste a great part of our lives imagining that we are inside, acting as if we were, and never achieving that depth where the kingdom of God unfolds in all its beauty, its truth and its glory.

When I say that we are intruders, outsiders, I do not mean to imply simply that a situation arises in which we are radically outside or radically inside. We should rather think in terms of from a growing progress from depth to depth, from height to height, whatever formula you prefer, so that to each step we already possess something that is rich, that is deep and, yet, we continue to desire more and move towards something richer and more deep. It is important to remember this, because we are extraordinariamente ricos incluso mientras estamos en el exterior.

God gives us so much, we are so rich intellectually and emotionally, our lives are so full that we can imagine that it may not there is nothing more than this, than feeling fulfilled and complete, that we have reached the end of our search. But we must know that there is always more. We should be glad that, as poor as We are, let us be so rich; but we must desire the true ones. riches of the Kingdom, to be careful not to be deceived by what is already We possess in such a way that we turn our backs to what remains ahead. about us.

We must remember that what we have is a gift. The first of the Blessed are the poor, and only if we live from agreement with this Beatitude we will be able to enter the kingdom of God. This Beatitude has two aspects. First, that it is perfectly clear that we possess nothing that we can keep, whether we want it or not; it is the discovery that I am nobody and of that I have nothing, total, irreparable, desperate poverty.
We exist because it has been desired that we exist and given existence.

We have done nothing for it, it has not been an act of our free will. will. We do not possess life in such a way that it is impossible to anyone can take it away from us, and everything we are and everything we own 14

it is so, ephemeral. We have a body, which will die. We have a mind; however, it is enough for a tiny glass to break in a brain so that the greater mind suddenly extinguishes. We have a heart, sensitive and alive and yet, at a certain moment in that we would like to lavish all our sympathy, all our understanding for someone who is in need of it, we have no more like a stone in our chest.

Thus, in a certain way, we can say that we own nothing because we are not owners of anything that is in our power. And this can lead us, not to feel belonging to the kingdom of God and to rejoice in it, but to despair, if we do not remember that, although none of these things are ours in such a way that no they can be taken from us, however, we are in possession of them.

This is the second aspect of the Beatitude. We are rich, and Everything we possess is a gift and a sign of God's love and Him the love of men is a continuous gift of divine love; and while we possess nothing, divine love manifests continually and totally.

But everything we take in our hands to possess comes from the kingdom of love. Certainly it becomes ours, but it is lost the love. And only those who give their all are aware of the true, total, final, irreparable spiritual poverty, and that they possess the love of God expressed in all his gifts. One of our Theologians have said: 'All the food of this world is divine love' edible." I believe it is true and at the moment we try to we are rich by keeping something safe in our hands, los losers, because while we have nothing in our hands, we can take, leave, do whatever we want.
This is the Kingdom, the feeling that we are free from possession, and this freedom establishes a relationship between us and everything that is love -- human love and divine love.

Now then, if we reason in these terms, we can transfer the same idea as what was said before. Yes, we are rich. However, we do not we should never be confused by what we possess until imagine that now we can demolish the old barns and build new ones to store more wealth. Nothing can be stored, 15
nothing but the kingdom of God itself. Thus we can discard a one thing after another to walk forward free, free to be rich.

Have you noticed that being rich always means a poverty at other levels? Just say: 'I have this watch, it is mine," and close your hand over it to take possession of a watch and having lost a hand. And if he closes his mind to his riches, if you close your heart to keep what is there safe, to never lose it, then it becomes as small as the thing in the one you have locked yourself in.

Well, if this is the case, at the moment you reach the bottom, in the moment when I become aware of the total detachment from all the things, then you find yourself on the edge of the kingdom of God, you are you are almost sure that God is love and that He holds you by His love. And at that point, you can say two things simultaneously.

You can pray from your complete misery, abandonment, and poverty and rejoice because you are rich in the love of God. But this is only if he has reached the point of discovering it, because while you imagine that you are rich there is nothing to be thankful for God, and you can't realize that you are loved. Too much to the formula for giving thanks that we use is an act in which we generalize, and the kind of repentance we offer to God is too much general regret.

I have experienced this once, in a somewhat unromantic way. not very spiritual. When I was a teenager, I remember going to a site and I had planned my trip very well because I was expecting that I would arrive at the time when people would be having lunch and I thought that if If I arrived on time, they possibly wouldn't have made me wait in the room. neighbor without giving me something to eat. But of course, my train was delayed. and I arrived after lunch and fiercely hungry. I was going with a friend and how we were really too hungry to we endured, we asked if they could give us anything. They told us: We have half a cucumber. We looked at the cucumber and looked at each other. mutually and we thought: Is this all that God can give us?

Then my friend said, 'And now let's give thanks to God.' I thought: "Thank you for a cucumber!" My friend was a better believer than I was and more. pious. We read some prayers, we read the prayer of thanksgiving. thank you and all the time I spent it with difficulty in detaching myself of the half cucumber, of which a quarter would be mine, and then we cut the cucumber and ate it. In my whole life, I have never felt 16

so grateful to God for no amount of food. I ate it how one would eat sacred food. I ate it carefully, to not to lose an ounce of the refreshing delight of cucumber and when we finished, I did not hesitate to say: "And now, let's give thanks to the Lord," and we did the thanksgiving sentences again.

We cannot live a life of prayer, we cannot move forward towards God, unless we are free from possessions to have two hands. what to offer and an absolutely open heart -- not like a bag that we fear to keep open because it might spill out money, but like an empty bag -- and an intelligence completely open to the unknown and unexpected. This is the way we are rich and completely free of wealth. This is the way we are rich and yet totally devoid of wealth. And this is the point at which we can talk about being outside the Kingdom and, nevertheless, to be so rich, to be inside and yet to be so free.

This is true, for example, when we fast. I'm not referring to the fasting and abstinence that affects not only the stomach but also the attitude of sobriety that allows you or pushes you to never be a slave of nothing. This is a matter of our overall conduct in life. It first of all, it affects our imagination because it is the point of departure of sin. One of our Orthodox writers from the century XIX said that the sins of the flesh are sins that the spirit it is a sin against the flesh. The flesh is not responsible and I think that In that sense, we must control our imagination. As long as our imagination does not dominate us, things are outside of us; once imagination gets tangled and falls prisoner to things we get stuck to things. You know that there are such things as meat and vegetables and pastries and all that. He knows it as such reality. Yes he sits down and says to himself: "I am not truly hungry, but there are so many pleasant things that I would like to eat, in five minutes there will be projected tentacles towards a great variety of things. It will be, like Gulliver, held to the ground by a hair and another hair and another hair; each of the hairs is not really important, but the sum total will keep you solidly tied to the ground. Once you have given their imagination complete freedom, things turn out better difficult. In this sense, we must be sober and we must fight for our freedom. There is a big difference between sympathy and love, between hunger and gluttony, between interest and curiosity and so on.
Each of our natural inclinations has a counterpoint. 17

that is marked by evil and that is one of the means by which we fall enslaved. This is what I mean by avoiding the tentacles. To begin, say "No". If "No" has not been said to time, one is in danger of having to fight. And in that case, be implacable because reason and detachment are more precious than what is obtained as a slave in terms of enjoyment.

Then, if what I have said so far is true, we must call for a door. At this moment some problems are serious. If the door It's from some church, we will go and call; But the difficulty lies in that we generally don't know where to call. How many times do people he wants to pray and wonders: "Well, where is the center of the Prayer? Where will I direct my gaze and heart? If it is you.
Muslim, it's simple. You turn towards Mecca. But even So, once we have oriented ourselves towards the East, what? I don't know.

You can focus on things that are less than God. In the moment when I tried to focus on an imaginary god or a god that you can imagine, runs the serious danger of placing an idol between you and the true God. This is an idea expressed in the age as early as the 4th century by Saint Gregory of Nazianzus.

He said that as soon as we put a visible sign before us, it is a crucifix, a tabernacle, an icon or an invisible image -- God as we imagine it, or Christ as we have seen in paintings -- and we focus our attention on it, so we have placed a barrier between us and God because we took the image we have formed by the person to whom we direct our prayers. What we must to do is to gather all the knowledge of God that we possess to go to his presence, but then, remember that everything we know about God comes from the past and we are standing face to face with God in all His complexity, all His simplicity, so close and so unknown. Only if we are completely open before it unknown, can the unknown reveal itself. Itself, how He chooses to reveal Himself to us as we are today. Thus, this open heart and open mind, we must appear before God without trying to give it a shape or imprison it in concepts and images and we must knock on a door.

Where? The Gospels tell us that the kingdom of God is within. us above all. If we cannot find the kingdom of God in it interior, in the deepest part of us, our possibilities of finding him outside is very unlikely. When Gagarin returned from the 18

space and made his famous statement that he had not seen God in the sky, one of our priests in Moscow replied: "If he has not seen never on earth, you will never find it in the sky. it is also true regarding what I am saying. If we do not find a contact with God within our own skin, whatever it may be, in this small world that I am, then the opportunities are very scarce that, even if I meet him face to face, I recognize him. San Juan Crisóstomo said: 'Find the door of your heart, you will discover' the door to the kingdom of God." So it is towards our interior that where we must turn and not outward, but inward of one in a very special way. I am not saying that we become introverts. I don't mean that we go inward in the manner What is done in psychoanalysis and psychology. It is not a journey to my inner self; it is a journey through myself to emerge from the deeper intimate level to the place where He is, the point in the that God and I meet.
So this issue of the incipient prayer has, assumption, two aspects: first, this internalization and, secondly place, the use of words in prayer and the direction towards what to guide them.

I will now talk about the second point. To whom or what will I direct myself? the sharp edge of my prayer? Often, people insist on shouting to the sky and is surprised to discover that the sky is empty and that it does not finds echo. It is not there where one can find echo. A writer spiritual of the 17th century, Saint John Climacus, said that a prayer, the Words in a sentence are like an arrow. But to possess one an arrow is not enough. If you want to hit a target, you have to have a a bow with a good string and a strong arm to draw it. If you have a good bow and if you can't draw it, your arrow will fall a few meters away. distance and it will not reach the target. If you do not stretch the bow with one arm powerful, you will not hit the target. What we need is the bow, the rope and the arm and the strength. Now, given that the words of the the arrow is the prayer, we head to the deepest point where God is within us; we can turn the bow towards Him interior and hit that deep point of our interior. Second Place, we must give the arrow all the conditions that will allow it cover your journey with the maximum power. Many times we are distracted in prayer, our heart is absent and our prayer is not supported by 19
our life. There, if you want, there are analogies with the bow, the string and the force.

There are moments when one can try to delve into the depths calling to Him who is at the origin and root of all the things, but he will see perfectly well where he is going and where to direct the sentence: not backward, not upward, but downward, deep, against all the resistances that appear along the way, against all the covered fallacies, against everything that hinders penetrate into the deepest depth. And so the prayer will be something perfectly feasible, though a tough, arduous, risky exercise.

First of all, then, we must choose a sentence. This is very important. Just as it is important to use the exact words if you maintains a relationship with someone, it is the same in the sentence. Be Whichever one we choose, it must be a sentence that makes sense. for us and a prayer that does not disturb us; I must admit that when I examine prayer manuals, I often I am troubled. It seems to me that if God were really present, specifically right in front of me, I would not dare to address him so coldly speeches and telling him things about himself that he has known for a long time before I came into the world. So it is necessary to choose, because if you are embarrassed by your prayer, God may feel it worried about you and the prayer as well and you will never be able to manage to get it to God with all my heart. The first thing, then, is truly find the words of the sentence that you deserve and God deserves. I say 'that you deserve and deserve God', because if if they are good enough for you, then God can accept, but if they are not good enough for you, just imagine for God. He has heard many better things than those. But no we must try to find words that may be in a certain way at the level of God. Unfortunately, as none of we are at God's level, we would meet halfway we would waste a large amount of time trying to find the right words.

Without intending to cover the entire topic, I would just like to give you a image of the merit of an act of worship or of words of worship. In the life of Moses, in Hebrew folklore, there is a página sobresaliente. Moisés encuentra a un pastor en el desierto.
Spend the day with the shepherd, help him milk his sheep, and at the end of the 20

One day, he sees the shepherd putting the best milk in a wooden bowl, that places on a flat stone at a certain distance. So Moses he asks why he does it and the pastor replies: 'It's the milk for God." Moses, confused, asks what it means. The shepherd I always take the best milk and offer it as a gift to God." Moses, who is much more sophisticated than the shepherd, with his faith naive, she asks: "And does God drink it?" "Yes," the pastor replies, "so So Moses felt compelled to enlighten the poor shepherd and he he explained that God, being pure spirit, does not drink milk. Without embargo, the pastor was sure that he did, so they held on a slight argument that ended when Moses proposed to the pastor that he/she would hide behind some bushes to find out if God was really going to drink her milk. Moses, in the meantime, withdrew. to pray in the desert. The shepherd hid, night came, and by the light of the moon, the shepherd saw a small fox trotting from the desert, I looked to the right and left and headed straight towards la leche, que poco después se tragaba, y desaparecía otra vez, en el desert. The next morning Moses found the shepherd quite depressed and disheartened. "What's going on?" he asked. The pastor replied He said: 'You were right, God is pure spirit and does not want milk.' Moses was surprised. He said: 'You should feel fortunate. You already know more about God of what you knew before." "Yes, I know," replied the pastor, "but I have lost the only thing I could do to express my love." Moses understood it. He withdrew to the desert and prayed for a long time.

At night he had a vision. God spoke to him and said: 'Moses, you were wrong. It is true that I am a pure spirit. However, I have always accepted with gratitude the milk that the shepherd was offering me, as expression of your love, but as a pure spirit I do not need the milk, I shared it with the little fox who likes it a lot.

I have tried to point out, first of all, that your prayer must become inward, not toward God in the sky nor toward God in the distance, but to God who is closer to you than you yourself; second place, that the first act of prayer is to choose the words that respond to what you are, words that you do not feel ashamed, that they represent him properly and are worthy of you, and then offer them to God with all the intelligence that you have capable. You must also put as much heart as you can into the act of worship, an act of recognition to God, an act of appreciation, What is the true meaning of charity, an action that encompasses 21
to the whole person in mind, heart, and an action that results suitable for you.

The first thing I suggest, therefore, is to ask yourself. what words of prayer seem meaningful to offer them to God, whether they are your own or those of others. Ask yourself in what way do they reach the heart, to what extent is he/she capable of mentally concentrate with them, because if not, you may not stay attentive to the words he says, why would God be? How could it Receiving them as an expression of love if you do not put your heart in them they have only put a certain dose of courtesy along with a certain degree of distraction?

And then, if you learn to say a chosen sentence in the moments in which you can give your full attention to the divine presence and offer this prayer to God, what happens gradually that the awareness of God increases within you so that whether he is among people, listening or talking or just working, that awareness is so strong that even being among people can pray. The analogy that some of our writers spiritual actions operate on two different levels: one is simpler and raw and, it seems to me, expresses very well what they are trying to say; the other is higher.
The simplest and rawest is something that one of our great guides of spirituality, Theophanes the Recluse, says: "The consciousness of God It should be with you as clearly as a toothache.

When you have a toothache, you don't forget it at all. You can being talking, reading, cleaning, singing, doing anything What; the toothache is continuously present and you can't escape from the presence of pain. He says that in the same way we must develop a pain in our hearts. I don't mean to say a physical pain, but rather in our vital center, be a pain like a desperate longing for God, a feeling that "I am alone," "Where is He?" expressed at the moment when you have lost. contact through prayer.

The highest way to express it, of course, is to say that when we feel great joy or great sorrow or great displeasure, we do not we forget throughout the day. We hear people, we make our own work, we read, we do what we must do and the feeling of affliction, the awareness of joy, of the stimulating news, 22

they are constantly within us. This should be the meaning of the presence of God. And if the sense of the presence of God is so clear like that, then one can pray while doing other things. One One can pray while doing manual work, but one can pray. when among other people, listening or maintaining a conversation or relationship. But, as I said, this does not happen at the beginning and I think we must learn to adopt an attitude of intense adoration and humility, conditions that allow it, because it is very easy to get distracted, to go from vigilance to drowsiness in the sentence. Let's learn this kind of watchful attitude in the prayer, of complete stability, of worship and submission to God, in moments when we can do so without distracting the mind or the heart, and then we can do it in other circumstances.
We will continue on this topic in the next chapter showing the the way one can take one or two sentences and use them to to delve deep into oneself to the place where God is.

In addition to this, I will try to explain how one can go inward, because this is another exercise. Don't forget the little fox; it can be very useful for your prayer life. And since we are on the topic of the foxes, if you want to know how to make friendship with God, learn of another fox in the book by Saint Exupery titled 'The Little Prince' prince," about the way to make friends, with one who is extremely sensitive, vulnerable, and shy.
